questions.text: Which enzyme begins carbohydrate digestion in the mouth?
questions.explanation: Salivary amylase breaks down starch into simpler sugars in the mouth.

questions.text: Where does most nutrient absorption occur in the alimentary canal?
questions.explanation: The small intestine has villi and microvilli specialized for absorption.
Bile emulsifies fats, increasing surface area for enzymes.

questions.text: Which organ secretes digestive enzymes into the small intestine?
questions.explanation: The pancreas releases enzymes like trypsin and lipase into the duodenum.

questions.text: Peristalsis is the movement of food by:
questions.explanation: Peristalsis uses coordinated muscle contractions along the gut.

questions.text: Which structure increases the surface area for absorption in the small intestine?
questions.explanation: Villi and microvilli increase the absorptive surface of the intestine.
